rationale: Gate-forwardness clears on all four axes. (a) ADR coverage: 0036+0032 (both Accepted, Domain(s) intersect workflow) cover workflow; 0033 (Accepted, context-system) covers context-system; ADR-0031 correctly NOT cited (its domains validation/doc-sync/lifecycle/ownership intersect neither impacted domain -> would trip adr-cite-irrelevant). No non-intersecting citation. (b) Every non-doc In-Scope path attributes at the future state: internal/{approve,validate,doctor,adr}/** -> workflow, internal/contextpack/** -> context-system, and newly-claimed internal/trace/** + .golangci.yml -> workflow; R10(a) same-diff-or-earlier ordering and R10(b) per-domain doc companion are complete. (c) Zero space-padded --- fences across all 207 repo spec/plan files, so R5's fence tightening is inert repo-wide; R6's approval-source change is warning-only, runs only over the ActiveSpec via CrossValidate (never iterates all specs) which is 108 with proper frontmatter at gate time, and next/mode.go + approve/plan.go already use the frontmatter reader so R6 merely makes state.go consistent. (d) validateReviewMode never calls readSpecApprovalStatus; R6's callers keep using root, so the .golangci validateReviewMode unparam carve-out stays valid. One non-blocking factual error: R1/R10 call .mindspec/domains/workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml 'non-doc source' -- it is doc-classified.
findings: NON-BLOCKING doc-accuracy error (R1, R10): the spec states `.mindspec/domains/workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml` is 'workflow-owned non-doc source' whose edit 'requires a same-diff workflow doc touch.' Verified against docsync.go: isDocFile matches `.mindspec/domains/` via lifecycleDocSubtrees, so it is DOC-classified; isProcessArtifact (divergence.go:293) therefore SKIPS it before attribution (never trips adr-divergence-unowned/uncovered); and it is itself a valid workflow domain-doc companion (hasArtifactPrefix(path,'domains/workflow') is true). Consequently a bead editing OWNERSHIP.yaml alongside workflow source is SELF-SATISFYING for checkInternalPackages -- no separate workflow doc is actually required. The constraint the spec bakes in is over-conservative, hence forward-SAFE (extra doc touches never fail a gate), so this is a wording fix, not a gate blocker. Recommend correcting R1/R10 to say OWNERSHIP.yaml is doc-classified and doubles as the workflow companion.

R6 behavioral change is genuinely disclosed and forward-safe. Scan of all spec.md shows the only prose-vs-frontmatter divergences are pre-frontmatter-era specs (005/008/015/021/026/... ) with prose Status: APPROVED but NO frontmatter at all (SpecStatus -> ""). None is ever the ActiveSpec during 108's lifecycle, and the migrated reader is confined to state.go:70/111 (CrossValidate, single ActiveSpec) -- no gate iterates all specs reading approval status. No existing spec relies on the divergence in a gate-breaking way.

R5 fence tightening: confirmed inert. No spec.md/plan.md in the repo (207 files) has a leading- or trailing-space-padded `---` fence, so switching from TrimSpace to canonical frontmatter.Parse semantics changes the parse of zero existing files, including every plan.md read by the divergence lane at complete/impl-approve.

ADR relevance/coverage gates will pass at plan-approve: all three cited ADRs are Accepted and each intersects an impacted-domain token; both impacted domains (workflow, context-system) are covered; ADR-0031's exclusion is the correct call.

Confirmed workflow OWNERSHIP already self-lists `.mindspec/domains/workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml` in paths (harmless: doc-classification pre-empts attribution regardless) and does NOT yet list internal/trace or .golangci.yml at base -- matching the spec's premise for work-area-1 ordering.
